An update from Wuxi Sunlit Science & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1289) ) is now available.

Wuxi Sunlit Science and Technology Company Limited, a PRC-based joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, operates in the science and technology equipment sector, supplying specialized products and services to industrial customers. The group’s business model centers on manufacturing and sales, with earnings attributable to shareholders reflecting performance in its targeted technical markets.

The company reported a sharp contraction in financial performance for the year ended 31 December 2025, with revenue dropping 64.3% to RMB184.4 million and gross profit down 62.3% to RMB54.0 million. Profit for the year fell 31.8% to RMB33.8 million, but results were partially supported by a net reversal of impairment on financial and contract assets and lower administrative expenses, helping to cushion the impact of weaker top-line performance on shareholders’ earnings.

Shengli Oil & Gas Pipe Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1080) ) just unveiled an update.

Shengli Oil & Gas Pipe Holdings Limited reported a sharp rise in revenue to about RMB903.2 million for 2025, up roughly RMB333.1 million from the previous year, as well as an improvement in gross profit margin to 12.0%. Despite remaining loss-making, the group narrowed its loss attributable to shareholders to about RMB18.9 million and reduced its total comprehensive loss, but the board opted against declaring interim or final dividends for the year.

The results show stronger operating performance supported by higher sales and improved profitability, partly offset by higher selling and administrative expenses and a litigation-related compensation charge. Finance costs eased slightly and contribution from an associate increased, signalling gradual operational recovery, though ongoing losses and the absence of a dividend highlight that balance sheet repair and cash preservation remain priorities for management and investors.

Ajisen (China) Holdings Limited has proposed a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.07 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, payable in Hong Kong dollars at HKD 0.0791 per share based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.13. data.title: Ganglong China Property Slumps to Deeper 2025 Loss as Revenue Collapses
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Ganglong China Property Group Limited ( (HK:6968) ) is now available.

Ganglong China Property Group reported a sharp deterioration in its 2025 results, with revenue plunging about 67% year on year to roughly RMB2.7 billion and the net loss nearly doubling to around RMB1.93 billion. Despite cutting selling, marketing, and administrative expenses and reducing bank and other borrowings by about 11% to RMB4.07 billion, the group suffered a large gross loss and higher finance costs and impairments, underscoring ongoing financial strain in its operations and signaling continued pressure on shareholders and creditors.

The company’s loss attributable to shareholders widened to approximately RMB1.29 billion, with basic and diluted loss per share increasing to RMB0.80, while non-controlling interests also absorbed a substantial share of the downturn. The combination of collapsing revenue, valuation losses on investment properties, weaker contributions from joint ventures and associates, and rising net finance costs highlights significant challenges to profitability and balance-sheet strength in a still-fragile Chinese property market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6968) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.08 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ganglong China Property Group Limited stock, see the HK:6968 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: EDA Group flags 2025 loss on overseas expansion and rising costs
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EDA Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2505) ) has provided an announcement.

EDA Group Holdings Limited has warned that it expects to swing from a net profit of about RMB47.1 million in 2024 to a net loss of roughly RMB12 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, with adjusted results shifting from a profit of RMB113.9 million to an adjusted net loss of around RMB8.5 million. The downturn is mainly attributed to upfront costs and amortisation from strategic expansion into new overseas warehouses, pressure on order unit prices from tariff policy changes and heightened competition, higher overseas logistics and labour expenses, and a larger provision for expected credit losses as receivables age, signalling near-term margin pressure even as management frames the warehouse investments as critical to sustaining long-term growth.

The board underscored that the adjusted net loss measure excludes listing and share-based payment expenses, which it views as non-operational or one-off items, to provide a clearer view of underlying performance. Final audited results for 2025 have yet to be completed and may differ from these preliminary figures, leaving investors to weigh short-term earnings weakness against the potential strategic benefits of the company’s expanded international logistics infrastructure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2505) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on EDA Group Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2505 Stock Forecast page.

More about EDA Group Holdings Limited

EDA Group Holdings Limited operates in the cross-border logistics and warehousing sector, focusing on overseas warehouse leasing and related logistics services for international trade customers. The group has been expanding its footprint by adding new leased overseas warehouses since the second half of 2024 to support long-term growth in global markets.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China ( (HK:1398) ) has issued an announcement.

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Limited’s board met in Beijing on March 27, 2026, in a duly convened session with full director participation, to approve several changes aimed at strengthening governance and compliance oversight. The meeting confirmed adjustments to special board committees and advanced key financial reporting matters for shareholder review.

The board appointed Dong Yang as a member and vice chairman of the Audit Committee and named Zhong Mantao as a member of the Nomination Committee, with both abstaining from votes on their own appointments in line with governance norms. In a further move to bolster regulatory oversight, the board resolved that vice chairman Liu Jun will concurrently serve as the bank’s chief compliance officer, a decision endorsed by independent non-executive directors as compliant with relevant laws and internal rules.

The board also approved the bank’s 2025 audited accounts, clearing the way for these financial statements to be submitted to the shareholders’ meeting for consideration, underscoring ICBC’s focus on formalizing its financial reporting and governance framework. These decisions collectively reinforce ICBC’s board structure, internal control environment and compliance function, with implications for regulatory confidence and oversight of the bank’s expanding operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1398) stock is a Buy with a HK$7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Industrial and Commercial Bank of China stock, see the HK:1398 Stock Forecast page.

More about Industrial and Commercial Bank of China

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Limited is a major Chinese state-owned commercial bank that provides a full range of banking and financial services to corporate, institutional and retail clients. Listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1398, the bank operates domestically and internationally, focusing on commercial lending, deposit-taking, and comprehensive financial services across key sectors of China’s economy.

China Youran Dairy Group Limited reported a modest 2.8% increase in revenue to RMB20.65 billion for the year ended December 31, 2025, with gross profit rising 6.4% to RMB6.15 billion, reflecting improved operational efficiency and stronger margins. Technovator International ( (HK:1206) ) has issued an update.

Technovator International announced boardroom changes effective 27 March 2026, with chairman and non-executive director Li Chengfu and non-executive director Zhang Yanhua resigning from the board and its committees due to work arrangement adjustments, with both stating they had no disagreement with the board. The company has appointed Han Tao as non-executive director, board chairman, committee member and authorised representative, and Gao Peifeng as non-executive director and risk committee member, signaling a refresh of its governance structure and potential strategic influence from Han’s management experience at Tongfang-linked entities and state-backed investment firms.

Han Tao, a senior engineer with degrees in nuclear engineering from Tsinghua University and more than a decade of corporate management and project administration experience, has signed a one-year, automatically renewable service contract with no initial director fee, subject to review and shareholder-authorised board adjustments. His lack of other listed directorships, roles within the group, or ties to major shareholders underscores an independent profile within the non-executive ranks, while the appointments collectively aim to maintain regulatory compliance and continuity in Technovator’s leadership amid the transitions.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1206) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Technovator International stock, see the HK:1206 Stock Forecast page.

Lesi Group Limited reported a sharp top-line expansion for 2025, with revenue surging 127.3% year-on-year to RMB1.55 billion and total gross billing jumping 172.7% to RMB2.73 billion, driven by the recovery in the macro economy and structural shifts in China’s consumer market. data.title: MMG Lifts 2025 Cap on Rosebery Concentrate Sales as Metal Prices Surge
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

MMG ( (HK:1208) ) has issued an update.

MMG has raised the 2025 annual cap on its continuing connected transaction for Rosebery concentrate sales to Minmetals North-Europe to US$150 million from a previously revised US$125 million, reflecting stronger-than-expected copper, gold and silver prices. The company said all 2025 shipments stayed within planned volumes and that higher metal prices are driving a larger final price adjustment, with independent directors deeming the new cap commercially fair and in shareholders’ interests, while internal controls and conflict-of-interest safeguards remain in place.

The revised cap, which follows an earlier increase from the original US$100 million limit, underlines MMG’s growing revenue exposure to favourable commodity price movements in its concentrate offtake arrangements. Management will continue to monitor transaction pricing and compliance with the cap, suggesting modest upside to 2025 revenue from the Rosebery contract while maintaining governance measures typical for connected transactions in Hong Kong’s listing regime.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1208) stock is a Buy with a HK$9.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MMG stock, see the HK:1208 Stock Forecast page.

More about MMG

MMG Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated resources company engaged in the production and sale of base and precious metal concentrates. data.title: AviChina Defines Audit and Risk Panel’s Mandate to Bolster Governance
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AviChina Industry & Technology Co ( (HK:2357) ) just unveiled an announcement.

AviChina Industry & Technology Company Limited operates in the aviation and defense manufacturing sector, focusing on aeronautical products, related technologies and industrial services. The group is listed in Hong Kong and is governed by the Company Law of the People’s Republic of China and the SEHK Listing Rules, which shape its corporate governance and oversight structure.

The company’s board has established an Audit and Risk Management Committee as a specialized sub-committee to oversee financial operations, auditing procedures and risk management. Comprised entirely of non-executive directors, with a majority being independent and at least one member holding professional accounting qualifications, the committee is mandated to monitor internal controls, liaise with internal and external auditors and assume certain supervisory functions to strengthen transparency and regulatory compliance.

The terms of reference set out the committee’s responsibilities for ongoing assessment of the effectiveness of internal control and risk management systems. They also prescribe membership criteria, rotation and confidentiality obligations, including restrictions on former partners of the company’s auditing firm, underscoring an effort to enhance independence, safeguard sensitive information and align with evolving regulatory requirements for listed entities.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Ltd ( (HK:0334) ) has provided an announcement.

China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Limited plans to overhaul its constitutional documents, proposing a new set of bye-laws to replace its existing framework. The move aims to better align the company’s governance structure with evolving regulatory requirements in Hong Kong’s capital markets and facilitate more modern modes of operation and communication.

The proposed amendments will bring the company in line with recent Listing Rules changes on uncertificated securities, expanded paperless listing arrangements and electronic dissemination of corporate communications, while also allowing it to hold repurchased shares as treasury stock. The package of changes, which includes consequential and housekeeping updates, is subject to shareholder approval by special resolution at the annual general meeting scheduled for 28 May 2026, signaling a further step toward digitalisation and capital management flexibility for the issuer.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0334) stock is a Buy with a HK$0.44 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Ltd stock, see the HK:0334 Stock Forecast page.

China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the display and optoelectronics sector. The group focuses on developing and manufacturing display-related technologies and products for consumer electronics and other applications, serving both domestic Chinese and international markets.

An announcement from SIIC Environment Holdings ( (SG:BHK) ) is now available.

SIIC Environment Holdings has scheduled its 2026 annual general meeting for 29 April 2026 at Conrad Singapore Marina Bay, where shareholders from both Singapore and Hong Kong are invited to attend in person and vote or appoint proxies. The company will distribute its FY2025 annual report, AGM circular, notice, and proxy form primarily via electronic channels, while offering printed materials only on request, in a move that underscores its sustainability push and aligns with market trends toward digital communication and enhanced shareholder engagement, including structured pre-meeting Q&A and published AGM minutes.

More about SIIC Environment Holdings

SIIC Environment Holdings is a Singapore-incorporated company listed in both Hong Kong and Singapore, operating in the environmental services sector. The group focuses on water and wastewater treatment and related environmental infrastructure, serving municipal and industrial clients across its key Asian markets.
